markdownRemark.html:
Through this short post, I'd like to look back on my blogging journey so far and recalibrate the direction going forward 🧐

It has already been a year and four months since I first created this blog. At the time, I was interested in web development and didn't plan to pursue it as a career, but I wanted to study it consistently for at least a year. So I set up this blog as a way to organize what I learned after taking a basic web development course, and the early posts were mostly about following along with and summarizing lecture content. Since there weren't many posts on the blog back then, I remember prioritizing quantity over quality as a way to motivate myself. It was only after a decent number of posts had accumulated that I started focusing on topics I wanted to remember for a long time.

After about a year of blogging, my interests have shifted from web development to machine learning theory, and I feel that the purpose and nature of my blog posts have changed from what they were at the start. Whereas before I was posting about web technologies and techniques needed to complete a project, I now plan to shift toward organizing theoretical content related to the field I'll be researching in graduate school 🥕.

So I plan to fill this blog primarily with content related to foundational mathematics and machine learning theory. Rather than posting about newly discovered concepts or topics, I intend to focus on content that was difficult to understand even after reading it two or three times, or content I want to remember even two or three years from now. (For that reason, these posts are still more for my own study purposes rather than explanations for others.) I aim to spend at least five hours or more on each post, and I'm thinking the posting frequency will be somewhere between once every two weeks to once a month.

When I wrote my very first blog post, I thought that keeping it up for more than three months would be a success—so I feel somewhat relieved (?) that I've managed to go longer than that. My goal going forward is to keep posting consistently throughout my remaining two years of graduate school. Let's go! 💪
